Google Gemini new free updates are insane. Google just dropped five massive updates to Gemini and they are completely free. We are talking AI inside Google Maps, AI inside Chrome, AI inside Docs, Sheets, Slides, and a brand new AI model that nobody is talking about. This is the biggest Gemini update yet. And by the end of this video, you are going to know exactly how to use every single one of them. So, let me break it all down. So, the first update is wild. Google put Gemini inside Google Maps. And I do not mean a little chatbot in the corner. I mean you can now talk to Maps like it is your personal travel guide. Here is what that looks like. You open Google Maps. You type something like plan a 2-day food tour in Tokyo. And Gemini does not just give you a list of restaurants. It actually reads through reviews, looks at photos, checks local guides, and builds you a full plan, stops, times, routes, everything. Or say you're in a new city. You type find a quiet cafe to work near a park. Gemini pulls up options based on what people actually said in their reviews. Not just star ratings, actual context. And here is where it gets even crazier. So they added something called immersive navigation. So now when you are driving or walking somewhere, Gemini can highlight landmarks, show you directions that feel almost like augmented reality, and even say things like, "Take the second right after the red building." That is next level. That is not just a map anymore. That is an AI co-pilot sitting in your passenger seat. Think about what this means for travel. You do not need to spend 3 hours on Reddit trying to plan a trip. You just ask maps. Done. This one feature alone is going to save people so much time. And again, it is free. Now, the second update is where things get really interesting for anyone who uses Google Workspace. And if you use Docs, Sheets, Slides, or Drive, pay attention because this changes everything. Gemini is now baked into all of them. Let me start with Google Docs. You can now tell Gemini to write entire documents for you. Like you open a blank doc, type write a blog post about AI automation and it just does it full thing. You can also highlight a paragraph and say rewrite this to sound more professional or summarize this whole report into three bullet points. It does it instantly. Google Sheets is even more impressive. You can now ask Gemini to analyze your spreadsheets. So say you have a big data set of sales numbers. You type find trends in this data and build me a chart. Gemini reads the data, spots the patterns, and creates the chart for you. No formulas, no pivot tables, just plain English. Google Slides now lets you generate entire presentations. You type create a 10 slide presentation about climate change and it builds the whole thing. Slides, design, layout, content, all of it. You can go from zero to a full presentation in seconds. And Google Drive got a huge upgrade, too. You can now search your files using normal language or summarize everything in this folder. Gemini reads through your files and gives you answers. No more digging through 50 folders trying to find that one document. This is not some future feature. This is live right now and it is free for Workspace users. Hey, if we have not met already, I am the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of Goldie Agency. Whilst he is helping clients get more leads and customers, I am here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. Now, update number three is the one that developers are losing their minds over. And honestly, even if you are not a developer, you need to understand this because it is going to change how every AI app works. Google just released something called Gemini Embedding 2. And I know that sounds technical, but let me make it simple. Before this update, if you wanted to build an AI app that could understand text, images, and video, you needed three separate AI models. One for text, one for images, one for video, and you had to somehow connect them all together. It was a mess. Now, with Gemini Embedding 2, one single model handles everything. Text, images, video, audio, documents, all of it. One model, one system. Done. And the capabilities are serious. It can process up to 8,000 tokens of text. It can handle six images at once. It can process 2 minutes of video. It even supports audio natively. And it can read through six pages of a PDF. But here is the part that blows my mind. You can mix inputs. So you can give it an image and some text at the same time or a video with a transcript and it understands the relationship between them. That is something that was almost impossible before. Why does this matter to you? Because every AI tool you use is about to get way smarter. search engines, recommendation systems, AI assistants, all of them can now understand multiple types of content at the same time. This is a massive leap forward. And if you are building anything with AI, this simplifies your entire setup. No more stitching together five different models. One model does it all. Now, real quick, if you are watching this and thinking, I want to actually use AI to grow my business and automate things. Then you need to check out the AI profit boardroom. It is the best place to learn how to scale your business, get more customers, and save hundreds of hours with AI automation, especially with tools like Google Gemini that we are talking about right now. I will drop the link in the description and the comments. Go check it out. All right, update number four is short but super important. Google just added usage caps to the Gemini API inside Google AI Studio. So if you are a developer using the Gemini API, you can now set a maximum limit on how much your project uses. You pick a cap and once you hit it, the API stops automatically. No surprises, no waking up to something you did not expect. This is huge for startups, huge for solo developers, huge for anyone who wants to experiment with AI without worrying about things getting out of control. You set your limit, you build your thing, and you stay in control. Simple. It sounds like a small feature, but trust me, developers have been begging for this. And now it is here. Update number five, Gemini is now built directly into Google Chrome. So now when you are browsing the internet, you have an AI assistant right there in your browser. You do not need to open a separate tab. You do not need to copy and paste text into some other tool. Gemini is just there. What can it do? A lot. You can ask it to summarize a web page. So say you land on a long research paper. Instead of reading the whole thing, you just ask Gemini, "Summarize this and you get the key points in seconds." You can ask it to explain articles in simpler terms. You can ask it questions about whatever website you're on. You can even use it to help you write emails right from your browser. Right now, it is rolling out in India, New Zealand, and Canada first. More regions are coming soon. But this is the direction Google is going. Gemini everywhere, not just in one app, everywhere. Now, let me take a step back and tell you why all of this matters. Google is not just updating a chatbot. They are turning Gemini into a full AI layer that sits on top of everything. Your browser, your documents, your maps, your spreadsheets, your presentations, your file storage, your developer tools, everything. This is not five separate features. This is one strategy. Gemini is becoming the operating system for AI. And the fact that most of these features are free is wild. Think about where we were 12 months ago. You had to go to a separate website to use AI. Now it is inside the tools you already use every single day. That is the shift. AI is not a tool you go to anymore. It is a tool that comes to you and this is just the beginning. Google is clearly going allin on this. So if you are not paying attention to these updates, you are falling behind and I do not want that for you.
